name: export_document
description: Assemble a multi-section document and export it as DOCX, PDF, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, or ODT. Saves the file to data/documents/ and returns a download_path. After a successful export, include the returned download_path as a Markdown link in your response: [📄 Filename.docx](/api/v1/documents/file_id) — the UI renders this as a download card. For XLSX (spreadsheets), use xlsx_sheets instead of sections. For all other formats, provide sections as ordered markdown content blocks. Supported formats: docx (Word, default), pdf, pptx (PowerPoint — headings become slide titles), xlsx (Excel — requires xlsx_sheets), html, odt.

title.description: Document title (H1 heading for pandoc formats; first sheet name for XLSX if not set in xlsx_sheets).

format.description: Output format. docx is best for editable reports. xlsx for tabular BA artifacts (requirements matrix, risk register). pptx for stakeholder presentations.

filename.description: Output filename stem (no extension). Spaces and special chars will be sanitised. Timestamp appended to ensure uniqueness.

sections.description: Ordered sections for docx/pdf/pptx/html/odt. Not used for xlsx.

heading.description: Section heading (H2). For pptx this becomes the slide title.

content.description: Section body in Markdown. May include image references as standard Markdown: ![alt](/api/v1/documents/file.svg)

xlsx_sheets.description: For xlsx format only. List of sheets to create.

rows.description: List of rows. Each row is a list of cell values.

download_path.description: Relative URL: /api/v1/documents/{file_id}. Embed as Markdown link in your response.
